zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Geoffrey de Cusack於1220年出生在愛爾蘭庫迪 米亞斯格勒亞德斯托時，他的父親Sir Adam Cusack32歲，母親Lucy de Ledwych28歲。他於1247年與Bridget Petit在愛爾蘭庫迪 米亞斯格勒亞德斯托結婚。他們至少是4個兒子的父母。他去世於1295年在愛爾蘭庫迪 米亞斯可洛納德享年75歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir Geoffrey de Cusack于1220年出生在爱尔兰庫迪 米亞斯格勒亞德斯托时，他的父亲Sir Adam Cusack32岁，母亲Lucy de Ledwych28岁。他于1247年与Bridget Petit在爱尔兰庫迪 米亞斯格勒亞德斯托结婚。他们至少是4个儿子的父母。他去世于1295年在爱尔兰庫迪 米亞斯可洛納德享年75岁。

nameMeanings.name: Cusack
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Aileen, Brigid, Eamon, Fergus, Seamus.

Irish (of Norman origin): habitational name from Cussac in Guienne, France, named with the Gallo-Roman personal name Cūcius or Cussius + the locative suffix -acum. The surname died out in England, but is common in Ireland, where it was imported at the time of the Anglo-Norman invasion in the 12th century. It has been Gaelicized as de Cíosóg.

variants.name: Cuzick
meanings: Irish and English (Worcestershire): variant of   Cusack  . This form of the surname is no longer found in Ireland and is rare in Britain.

variants.name: Cusick
meanings: Irish and Scottish: variant of   Cusack  , now found predominantly in Scotland.
Americanized form of Polish Kusik or Czech Kusík, Slovak Kúšik and Kušík.
Americanized form of Croatian Kusić, Croatian and Serbian Kušić (see Kusic ), or Serbian Ćušić (see Cusic ).

nameMeanings.name: Geoffrey
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Of Germanic (Frankish and Lombard) origin, introduced to Britain by the Normans. It was in regular use among the counts of Anjou, ancestors of the English royal house of Plantagenet, who were descended from Geoffrey Plantagenet, Count of Anjou ( 1113–51 ). It was a particularly popular name in England and France in the later Middle Ages; notable bearers in England include the poet Geoffrey Chaucer ( c.1340–1400 ) and in Wales the chronicler Geoffrey of Monmouth ( d. 1155 ). The original form and meaning of the name is disputed. According to one theory, it is merely a variant of Godfrey ; others derive the first part from the Germanic word gawia ‘territory’, walah ‘stranger’, or gisil ‘pledge’. Medieval forms can be found to support all these theories, and it is possible that several names have fallen together, or that the name was subjected to reanalysis by folk etymology at an early date.

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of the various boys' names with the first syllable Ed-, especially Edward .

variants.name: Edward
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + weard ‘guard’. This has been one of the most successful of all Old English names, in frequent use from before the Conquest to the present day, and even being exported into other European languages. It was the name of three Anglo-Saxon kings and has been borne by eight kings of England since the Norman Conquest. It is also the name of the youngest son of Queen Elizabeth II . The most influential early bearer was King Edward the Confessor ( ?1002–66 ; ruled 1042–66 ). In a troubled period of English history, he contrived to rule fairly and (for a time at any rate) firmly. But in the latter part of his reign he paid more attention to his religion than to his kingdom. He died childless, and his death sparked off conflicting claims to his throne, which were resolved by the victory of William the Conqueror at the Battle of Hastings. His memory was honoured by Normans and English alike, for his fairness and his piety. Edward's mother was Norman; he had spent part of his youth in Normandy; and William claimed to have been nominated by Edward as his successor. Edward was canonized in the 12th century, and came to be venerated throughout Europe as a model of a Christian king.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Geoffrey de Cusack was born in 1220, in Gerrardstown, County Meath, Ireland, his father, Sir Adam Cusack, was 32 and his mother, Lucy de Ledwych, was 28. He married Bridget Petit in 1247, in Gerrardstown, County Meath, Ireland. They were the parents of at least 4 sons. He died in 1295, in Clonard, County Meath, Ireland, at the age of 75.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir Geoffrey de Cusack est né en 1220, à Gerrardstown, County Meath, Irlande, son père, Sir Adam Cusack, avait 32 ans et sa mère, Lucy de Ledwych, avait 28 ans. Il a épousé Bridget Petit en 1247, à Gerrardstown, County Meath, Irlande. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils. Il est décédé en 1295, à Clonard, County Meath, Irlande, à l’âge de 75 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir Geoffrey de Cusack 1220 in Gerrardstown, County Meath, Irland geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sir Adam Cusack 32 und seine Mutter Lucy de Ledwych 28. Er hat 1247 in Gerrardstown, County Meath, Irland Bridget Petit geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne. Er starb 1295 im Alter von 75 Jahren in Clonard, County Meath, Irland.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Geoffrey de Cusack è nato il nel 1220 nel Gerrardstown, County Meath, Irlanda, il suo padre, Sir Adam Cusack, aveva 32 anni e il suo madre, Lucy de Ledwych, aveva 28 anni. Lui sposato Bridget Petit nel 1247, nel Gerrardstown, County Meath, Irlanda. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli. Morì nel 1295, nel Clonard, County Meath, Irlanda, all'età di 75 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir Geoffrey de Cusackが1220年にアイルランドコウントイ メアスジャーラルドストウンで生まれた時，父Sir Adam Cusackは32歳，母Lucy de Ledwychは28歳であった。1247年にアイルランドコウントイ メアスジャーラルドストウンでBridget Petitと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子の親であった。彼は1295年にアイルランドコウントイ メアスクロナルドで75歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Geoffrey de Cusack(이)가 1220년 아일랜드 카운티 미스 그라르드스타운에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir Adam Cusack(은)는 32세였고, 그의 어머니 Lucy de Ledwych(은)는 28세였다. 그는 1247년 아일랜드 카운티 미스 그라르드스타운에서 Bridget Petit(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1295년 아일랜드 카운티 미스 클론아르드에서 75세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Geoffrey de Cusack nasceu em 1220, em Gerrardstown, County Meath, Irlanda, seu pai, Sir Adam Cusack, tinha 32 anos e sua mãe, Lucy de Ledwych, tinha 28 anos. Ele casou-se com Bridget Petit em 1247, em Gerrardstown, County Meath, Irlanda. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos. Ele faleceu em 1295, em Clonard, County Meath, Irlanda, com 75 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir Geoffrey de Cusack родился в 1220 г. в местности джиррардстоун, коунти мит, Ирландия, его отцу по имени Sir Adam Cusack было 32 года, а матери, которую звали Lucy de Ledwych, 28. Он женился на женщине по имени Bridget Petit в 1247 г. в местности джиррардстоун, коунти мит, Ирландия. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына. Он умер в 1295 г. в местности клонард, коунти мит, Ирландия в возрасте 75 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir Geoffrey de Cusack nació en 1220, en Gerrardstown, County Meath, Irlanda, su papá, Sir Adam Cusack, tenía 32 años y su mamá, Lucy de Ledwych, tenía 28 años. Se casó con Bridget Petit en 1247, en Gerrardstown, County Meath, Irlanda. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos. Murió en 1295, en Clonard, County Meath, Irlanda, a la edad de 75 años.
